repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Stat entry studies no-solution NPIV projection functionals; closest repo Stat entries are stat_ate_overlap_decay and stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap, both different minimax targets.

published_prior_art.cite: ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV defines least-squares no-solution inverse-problem targets but gives achievability/inference, not this minimax converse; ChenReiss2011RateOptimalityIllPosedEconometrics gives whole-function NPIR/NPIV minimax rates, not no-solution projection-functional moduli. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24660, https://arxiv.org/abs/0709.2003.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo entry gives a spectral-cutoff upper theorem for no-solution NPIV projection functionals.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR already gives dimension-reduction/thresholding upper rates for classical exact-solution NPIR linear functionals; ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV gives no-solution Tikhonov learners. The cutoff estimator is new mainly as the upper half of the proposed no-solution modulus. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961, https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24660.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal has polynomial/exponential no-solution NPIV functional phase diagrams.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenReiss2011RateOptimalityIllPosedEconometrics and BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR contain analogous mild/severe ill-posedness rate formulae for classical targets; the no-solution projection target is not covered. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/0709.2003, https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo Stat entry records this sanity reduction for the proposed no-solution NPIV target.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR is exactly the classical exact-solution NPIR linear-functional comparator that this proposition reduces to. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961.

repo_prior_art.cite: Closest archived spectral/Tikhonov analogues, eid_proximal_phase_v1 and q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov, are identification/spectral cautionary records rather than no-solution NPIV minimax-rate comparisons.

published_prior_art.cite: ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV discusses Tikhonov saturation and source-condition rates, and BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023SourceConditionDR uses higher-qualification/iterated Tikhonov ideas, but neither proves a no-solution intrinsic-modulus gap for first-order Tikhonov. Sources: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.24660, https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.13793.

novelty_flags.label: proposal comparator positioning
novelty_flags.one_line: The drafter omitted the required comparator-promise table while the prose positions the kernel against Shen et al., Breunig-Johannes, and Chen-Reiss, so the strict comparator chain is not auditable from the core.

novelty_flags.label: flagship-aspirant minimax and Tikhonov claims
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship-aspirant statements cite Shen et al., Chen-Reiss, and Breunig-Johannes without section/equation anchors tying the claimed missing converse and Tikhonov rate to exact published results.

soundness_flags.label: def:intrinsic-rate / thm:modulus-lower / thm:cutoff-upper
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposed r_n^star replaces the actual fixed-representer variance term n^{-1/2}(sum_{j<=m} psi_j^2/sigma_j^2)^{1/2} by a sigma-only saturated envelope, which is not sharp or even determined under only ass:source-psi.

soundness_flags.label: prop:closed-form-rates / prop:exact-solution-reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: The closed-form phase diagram and exact-solution reduction require a two-sided or exact representer-coefficient regime; the current ellipsoid-only source condition also allows zero or sparse psi, giving different rates.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:tikhonov-gap
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The full first-order-Tikhonov comparison is accepted only as a conditional conjecture after the modulus and cutoff upper bound are corrected and proved.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Strict suboptimality is bound to subclasses where the corrected cutoff risk is o(the precisely cited Shen et al. first-order Tikhonov rate).

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace r_n^star by a psi-dependent modulus using the actual coefficients psi_j, or add an explicit two-sided exact-source/anti-sparsity condition on psi that justifies the sigma-only phase diagram.

Add a comparator-promise table and location anchors: Shen et al. Eq. (4)/(5), Assumptions 3/4, Lemma 4, and Remark 10; Breunig-Johannes' linear-functional minimax theorem; Chen-Reiss' whole-function minimax theorem.

State the Tikhonov-gap conjecture as an explicit inequality between the corrected r_n^star and the exact Shen et al. first-order Tikhonov rate, with the subclass restrictions written as rate conditions rather than prose.
Add Hall-Horowitz 2006 and Chen-Christensen 2015 as secondary NPIV minimax/inference comparators to make clear that the novelty is no-solution projection geometry, not generic ill-posed-rate algebra.

recommended_next_step: Revise this angle: the no-solution NPIV modulus kernel is plausibly field-level and novel, but the rate object and comparator anchoring need repair before Stage 0.
